fullCQData.stem: Mr. Rony and Mr. Jony are two customers of Kortoya Bank Ltd. The bank provided them with two plastic cards. Even without cash, they can shop at malls using these cards. Although Rony had money in his account, Jony did not have any money in his account, yet Jony was able to complete his shopping.

questions.label: a
questions.text: What is mobile banking?
questions.answer: Mobile banking is a service provided by a bank or other financial institution that allows its customers to conduct financial transactions remotely using a mobile device such as a smartphone or tablet.

questions.label: b
questions.text: Why is a credit card better than a debit card? Explain.
questions.answer: A credit card is often considered better because it allows for "buy now, pay later," provides emergency funds when the account is empty, and often offers rewards, insurance, and higher security for online transactions.

questions.label: c
questions.text: What type of plastic card enabled Mr. Jony to shop? Explain in light of the stem.
questions.answer:
Data: Mr. Jony had "no money in his account" but successfully completed his shopping.
Analysis: The only plastic card that allows a customer to spend money they do not currently have (by using a pre-approved credit limit) is a Credit Card.
Conclusion: Mr. Jony was able to shop due to a 'Credit Card'.

questions.label: d
questions.text: Which of the two plastic cards discussed in the stem is excellent? Analyze.
questions.answer:
Data: Rony used a Debit Card (personal funds) and Jony used a Credit Card (borrowed funds).
Analysis: While a Debit Card is safe, a Credit Card is a powerful financial tool that offers a grace period for payment and enhances purchasing power.
Evaluation: In a modern economy, the flexibility of the Credit Card makes it the "excellent" choice for managing cash flow.
Decision: The 'Credit Card' is the superior tool as it facilitates transactions even during a personal cash crunch.

fullCQData.stem: Nabila and Shakila are two sisters. Both have bank accounts at Meghna Bank PLC. The bank provided both sisters with plastic cards. Both went shopping at a mall. Even though they had no cash, they were able to shop using the plastic cards. In this case, Nabila had money in her bank account, but Shakila had no money deposited in her account.

questions.label: a
questions.text: What is e-banking?
questions.answer: E-banking (Electronic Banking) refers to the automated delivery of new and traditional banking products and services directly to customers through electronic, interactive communication channels.

questions.label: b
questions.text: How has the Automated Clearing House (ACH) system simplified inter-bank transactions? Explain.
questions.answer: The Automated Clearing House (ACH) simplifies inter-bank transactions by electronically processing high-volume batches of credit and debit transfers (like payroll or bill payments) automatically, eliminating the need for manual check clearing and reducing time and errors.

questions.label: c
questions.text: Which plastic card of the bank did Shakila use?
questions.answer:
Data: Shakila was able to complete her shopping even though she had "no money deposited in her account."
Analysis: A plastic card that allows a user to spend up to a pre-approved limit regardless of their current account balance is a Credit Card.
Conclusion: Shakila used a 'Credit Card'.

questions.label: d
questions.text: Which of the plastic cards mentioned in the stem has relatively more advantages and facilities? Describe.
questions.answer:
Data: Nabila used a Debit Card (linked to balance) and Shakila used a Credit Card (linked to a loan limit).
Analysis: A Credit Card offers emergency funds, "buy now pay later" facilities, international usage, and reward points, whereas a Debit Card is limited to existing savings.
Evaluation: While a Debit Card is safer for avoiding debt, the Credit Card provides superior financial flexibility.
Decision: The 'Credit Card' offers more advantages because it acts as an instant source of credit during financial emergencies.

fullCQData.stem: Mr. Mukit opened an account at 'Kheya Bank,' where he can perform all types of banking transactions at any branch of the bank within banking hours in a specific region. On the other hand, Mr. Faruk opened an account at 'Trisha Bank,' where he can perform banking activities at any time and any place through a website.

questions.label: a
questions.text: What is SWIFT?
questions.answer: SWIFT (Society for Worldwide Interbank Financial Telecommunication) is a global provider of secure financial messaging services that allows banks to send and receive information about financial transactions in a standardized and secure environment.

questions.label: b
questions.text: How can banking activities be performed from home? Explain.
questions.answer: Banking activities can be performed from home using Internet Banking or Mobile Banking apps. Customers can check balances, transfer funds, pay utility bills, and open accounts using a computer or smartphone connected to the internet.

questions.label: c
questions.text: What type of banking service is 'Kheya Bank' providing in the stem? Discuss.
questions.answer:
Data: Mr. Mukit can transact from "any branch" of the bank within a specific region.
Analysis: A system where a customer can access their account from any branch of the same bank, rather than just their home branch, is called 'Online Banking' or 'Any Branch Banking'.
Conclusion: 'Kheya Bank' is providing 'Online Branch Banking' services.

questions.label: d
questions.text: Mr. Faruk's acceptance of banking services is more significant than Mr. Mukit's—Describe this statement.
questions.answer:
Data: Mr. Mukit is limited to "banking hours" and "specific regions," while Mr. Faruk can transact "anytime and anywhere" via a website.
Analysis: Website-based banking (Internet Banking) removes geographical and temporal barriers completely.
Evaluation: Mr. Faruk enjoys 24/7 access and can transact internationally or from home, which is far more efficient than visiting a physical branch.
Decision: The statement is true; Internet Banking (Faruk) is more significant as it provides total mobility and time-saving convenience compared to branch-based online banking (Mukit).

fullCQData.stem: The officials of Kazi Ltd. are performing their official activities from home due to the emergence of a new virus. To ensure timely salary payments, the financial manager advised opening bank accounts in their own names as well as accounts in Nagad, Rocket, and bKash. Their salaries will be deposited into these accounts at the end of the month.

questions.label: a
questions.text: What is the full form of SWIFT?
questions.answer: The full form of SWIFT is 'Society for Worldwide Interbank Financial Telecommunication'.

questions.label: b
questions.text: Why is a credit card better than a debit card?
questions.answer: A credit card is often considered better because it allows for emergency spending when the account balance is zero and provides a grace period for repayment, whereas a debit card requires an immediate balance.

questions.label: c
questions.text: What type of banking is mentioned in the stem? Explain.
questions.answer:
Data: The officials are working from home and using digital platforms like Nagad, Rocket, bKash, and bank accounts for salary transfers.
Analysis: The use of digital technology and mobile platforms to conduct financial transactions remotely is called Electronic Banking (E-banking) and Mobile Financial Services (MFS).
Conclusion: The stem refers to 'Mobile Banking' and 'Internet Banking'.

questions.label: d
questions.text: Analyze how the banking system mentioned in the stem has made financial transactions easy and safe.
questions.answer:
Data: Transactions are happening remotely via digital accounts.
Analysis: These systems remove the need for physical cash handling, which reduces the risk of theft or loss.
Evaluation: Digital records ensure transparency, and encryption provides security, while the ability to pay instantly from anywhere makes it highly efficient.
Decision: The E-banking and MFS systems mentioned have significantly enhanced safety by providing a cashless environment and simplified transactions through instant, 24/7 accessibility.

fullCQData.stem: Mr. Ratan went to Cox's Bazar sea beach for Eid vacation with his family. While shopping, he realized he had no cash and his bank account balance was also insufficient. He then paid for his purchases using a plastic card he had with him. Impressed by this facility, he wanted to get a card that he could also use abroad for withdrawals and shopping. Upon contacting the bank, they provided him with such a card.

questions.label: a
questions.text: What is Point of Sale (POS) service?
questions.answer: Point of Sale (POS) service is a system where a customer makes a payment for goods or services at a retail location using a plastic card (debit/credit) through a specialized terminal.

questions.label: b
questions.text: Has e-banking simplified bank activities?
questions.answer: Yes, e-banking has simplified bank activities by automating transactions, reducing paperwork, providing 24/7 access, and allowing customers to manage funds from anywhere without visiting a physical branch.

questions.label: c
questions.text: What type of card did Mr. Ratan use to pay for his shopping in Cox's Bazar? Explain.
questions.answer:
Data: Mr. Ratan paid for shopping even though he had "insufficient balance" in his account.
Analysis: A card that allows a user to spend beyond their account balance using a pre-approved loan limit is a Credit Card.
Conclusion: Mr. Ratan used a 'Credit Card'.

questions.label: d
questions.text: Which of the cards mentioned in the stem has more extensive services? Give arguments in favor.
questions.answer:
Data: The first card was a local Credit Card; the second card allows usage "outside the country."
Analysis: A card that can be used internationally is typically a Dual Currency or Global Credit Card.
Evaluation: International cards offer global mobility, currency conversion, and worldwide merchant acceptance.
Decision: The second card (International/Global Credit Card) has more extensive services as its scope is global rather than limited to domestic boundaries.

fullCQData.stem: The use of checks is everywhere; everyone, including businessmen, likes them. A major advantage of bank checks is that a check from one branch can be encashed at another. Therefore, businessman Mr. Kamrul has significantly reduced cash transactions. He uses a type of magnetic plastic card and is now free from some of the problems associated with using checks.

questions.label: a
questions.text: What is e-banking?
questions.answer: E-banking (Electronic Banking) is the automated delivery of traditional and new banking products and services directly to customers through electronic communication channels.

questions.label: b
questions.text: Why is mobile banking popular?
questions.answer: Mobile banking is popular because it provides 24/7 access to banking services from anywhere via a smartphone, is cost-effective, and allows for instant fund transfers and bill payments.

questions.label: c
questions.text: What type of banking facility is Mr. Kamrul receiving from his bank? Explain.
questions.answer:
Data: Mr. Kamrul can encash checks from any branch and uses a magnetic plastic card for transactions.
Analysis: A system where banking services are accessible across all branches of a bank network is called 'Online Banking' or 'Any Branch Banking'.
Conclusion: Mr. Kamrul is receiving 'Online Banking' services.

questions.label: d
questions.text: Mr. Kamrul is now free from the problems of using checks—Analyze the statement.
questions.answer:
Data: Checks have risks like being misplaced, signature mismatch, or being "stale."
Analysis: Plastic cards (Debit/Credit) provide instant verification, PIN security, and 24/7 ATM access.
Evaluation: Unlike checks, cards do not require physical clearing time or manual verification at a counter.
Decision: The statement is true; by using plastic cards and online banking, he avoids the delays, risk of forgery, and physical carrying of checkbooks.

fullCQData.stem: Banking services and facilities have improved immensely. In the past, receiving banking services was time-consuming and expensive, but now it has become time and cost-efficient. Keeping this in mind, Mr. Mamun collected a card from the bank through which he can withdraw money, transfer funds, and purchase goods and services against his account balance. On the other hand, Mr. Kashem collected a card that provides all the above facilities plus a loan facility up to a certain limit.

questions.label: a
questions.text: What is One-Stop Service?
questions.answer: One-Stop Service is a banking facility where a customer can receive all types of services (deposits, withdrawals, etc.) from a single counter or point of contact.

questions.label: b
questions.text: What is the reason for the decrease in the use of paper currency in the e-banking world? Explain.
questions.answer: The use of paper currency is decreasing because e-banking offers safer, faster, and more convenient digital alternatives like cards, mobile apps, and internet transfers that eliminate the need for physical cash.

questions.label: c
questions.text: What type of card is used by Mr. Mamun in the stem? Explain.
questions.answer:
Data: Mr. Mamun's card allows withdrawals and shopping "against the balance" kept in his account.
Analysis: A card that is directly linked to the user’s bank balance and does not allow spending beyond it is a Debit Card.
Conclusion: Mr. Mamun is using a 'Debit Card'.

questions.label: d
questions.text: Compare and analyze the facilities of the two cards mentioned in the stem.
questions.answer:
Data: Mamun has a Debit Card; Kashem has a card with an "additional loan facility" (Credit Card).
Analysis: A Debit Card uses existing savings (no debt), while a Credit Card offers a grace period for payment and emergency funds.
Evaluation: While both facilitate cashless shopping, the Credit Card provides extra purchasing power during a cash crunch.
Decision: Mr. Kashem's Credit Card is superior in terms of financial flexibility, whereas Mr. Mamun's Debit Card is superior for disciplined budget management.

fullCQData.stem: Humaira can withdraw money deposited in the bank at any time using a magnetic card provided by the bank. On the other hand, Najifa can use a similar but different type of card to make all purchases and pay bills even though she does not have sufficient money in her bank account. However, Najifa has to pay that money back to the bank with interest later.

questions.label: a
questions.text: What is Automated Clearing House service?
questions.answer: Automated Clearing House (ACH) is an electronic network for financial transactions that processes large volumes of credit and debit batches, such as payroll and bill payments, automatically.

questions.label: b
questions.text: Why is mobile banking popular? Explain.
questions.answer: Mobile banking is popular because it provides 24/7 access to banking services from anywhere, allows instant fund transfers, is cost-effective, and eliminates the need for physical visits to a bank branch.

questions.label: c
questions.text: What type of magnetic card is used by Humaira in the stem? Explain.
questions.answer:
Data: Humaira can withdraw only the money "deposited in the bank."
Analysis: A magnetic card that is linked to a bank account and allows the holder to spend or withdraw only up to the available balance is a Debit Card.
Conclusion: Humaira is using a 'Debit Card'.

questions.label: d
questions.text: Between the two cards mentioned in the stem, which card's facility is relatively higher? Give arguments in favor of your answer.
questions.answer:
Data: Humaira uses a Debit Card (linked to balance); Najifa uses a Credit Card (linked to a loan facility).
Analysis: A Credit Card provides emergency liquidity, allows shopping without a balance, and offers grace periods for repayment.
Evaluation: While a Debit Card avoids debt, the Credit Card serves as an essential tool during a cash crunch or medical emergency.
Decision: The 'Credit Card' has relatively higher facilities because it acts as a revolving credit line and provides financial flexibility that a Debit Card cannot.

fullCQData.stem: When Mr. Muhin's father suddenly fell ill, 25,000 BDT was urgently needed for treatment. When he asked his friend Shahin for a loan, Shahin didn't have cash either, but he quickly went out with a card and collected the money. Mr. Shahin often travels abroad for business. In that case, he sometimes needs extra money. Therefore, he returned his previous card to the bank and collected a new one. As a result, he is getting more facilities from the new card instead of the old one.

questions.label: a
questions.text: What is Ready Cash?
questions.answer: Ready Cash is a specialized banking service (often associated with specific banks) that allows customers to withdraw cash instantly from ATMs or authorized points using a card.

questions.label: b
questions.text: Why is e-banking important in ensuring customer service?
questions.answer: E-banking is important because it ensures 24/7 service, reduces transaction time, provides real-time updates, and allows customers to perform complex banking tasks from the comfort of their homes or offices.

questions.label: c
questions.text: How did Mr. Shahin collect the money in the stem? Explain.
questions.answer:
Data: Shahin did not have cash but used a "card" to "quickly collect money" for a medical emergency.
Analysis: The device used to withdraw cash instantly using a plastic card is an Automated Teller Machine (ATM).
Conclusion: Mr. Shahin collected the money through an 'ATM' using either a Debit or Credit card.

questions.label: d
questions.text: How much more convenient do you think the card Mr. Shahin collected later is compared to the first card? Give your opinion.
questions.answer:
Data: The first card was likely a local debit/credit card; the second card is for "abroad" with "extra money" facilities.
Analysis: An International/Dual Currency Credit Card allows for global spending and provides a credit limit that acts as a buffer.
Evaluation: The second card removes geographical barriers and provides a safety net for business travel.
Decision: The new card is significantly more convenient as it offers global liquidity and a credit line, which the first (likely local debit) card lacked.

fullCQData.stem: Mr. Hasan has a savings account in Alpha Bank. For ease of transactions, the bank authority issued him an electronic card with which he can withdraw money as well as shop. On the other hand, his friend Mr. Hossain uses an electronic card that provides loan facilities and allows purchasing goods on credit for a specific period.

questions.label: a
questions.text: What is Retained Earnings?
questions.answer: Retained Earnings refers to the portion of a company's net income that is kept by the business rather than being distributed to shareholders as dividends, usually for reinvestment.

questions.label: b
questions.text: How does a bank assist in business transactions? Explain.
questions.answer: Banks assist in business transactions by providing various payment mechanisms (checks, L/Cs, electronic transfers), offering credit facilities, managing foreign exchange, and acting as a guarantor for trade.

questions.label: c
questions.text: What type of card did Alpha Bank issue to Mr. Hasan? Explain.
questions.answer:
Data: Mr. Hasan uses the card for "withdrawals and shopping" linked to his "savings account."
Analysis: An electronic card issued against a savings account that allows the holder to access their own funds for cash or purchases is a Debit Card.
Conclusion: Alpha Bank issued Mr. Hasan a 'Debit Card'.

questions.label: d
questions.text: Between the two cards mentioned in the stem, which one seems better to you? Analyze.
questions.answer:
Data: Hasan has a Debit Card; Hossain has a Credit Card (loan/credit facility).
Analysis: A Debit Card is excellent for debt-free living and tracking personal savings. A Credit Card is superior for managing cash flow and emergencies.
Evaluation: In a business-oriented or emergency-prone context, the Credit Card’s ability to provide funds when the account is empty is a major advantage.
Decision: The 'Credit Card' is better in terms of utility and emergency support, though the 'Debit Card' is better for personal financial discipline.

fullCQData.stem: Mr. Russel is a customer of 'Alpha Bank Ltd.' To perform banking transactions easily, he uses an electronic card instead of checks. On the other hand, his brother Mr. Foysal uses an electronic card through which he can withdraw money, take loans, and make deposits, among other financial transactions.

questions.label: a
questions.text: What is mobile banking?
questions.answer: Mobile banking is a service provided by a bank or financial institution that allows customers to conduct financial transactions remotely using a mobile device such as a smartphone.

questions.label: b
questions.text: Insecurity is the main obstacle to electronic banking—Explain.
questions.answer: Electronic banking relies entirely on digital networks. If these networks are compromised by hacking, phishing, or technical glitches, customers risk losing their funds and privacy, making security concerns the biggest barrier to widespread adoption.

questions.label: c
questions.text: What type of electronic card is used by Mr. Russel in the stem? Explain.
questions.answer:
Data: Mr. Russel uses a card "instead of checks" to perform basic transactions easily.
Analysis: A basic electronic card used for withdrawals and payments directly from an account balance is a Debit Card.
Conclusion: Mr. Russel's electronic card is a 'Debit Card'.

questions.label: d
questions.text: How can the electronic card used by Mr. Foysal play a role in business and trade? Analyze.
questions.answer:
Data: Mr. Foysal uses a card that allows "taking loans" (credit) and other financial transactions.
Analysis: This refers to a Credit Card. In business, it provides instant short-term credit, facilitates international trade payments, and helps manage cash flow during periods of low liquidity.
Decision: Mr. Foysal's 'Credit Card' acts as a revolving credit line, allowing businesses to purchase raw materials or pay bills immediately without waiting for their own receivables, thus accelerating the trade cycle.

fullCQData.stem: Mr. Johirul Islam has been involved in business for a long time. To modernize his business activities, he connected with internet banking. As a result, he can complete financial transactions with domestic and foreign parties in a short time. His business is expanding day by day.

questions.label: a
questions.text: What is online banking?
questions.answer: Online banking is a system that allows customers to perform banking transactions, such as fund transfers and balance inquiries, over the internet through a bank's secure website.

questions.label: b
questions.text: What type of card is a VISA Card? Explain.
questions.answer: A VISA Card is a globally recognized payment card (can be Debit, Credit, or Prepaid) that uses the Visa network to process transactions at millions of merchants and ATMs worldwide.

questions.label: c
questions.text: What types of benefits can Mr. Johirul Islam get by using the internet in his business as described in the stem? Explain.
questions.answer:
Data: Mr. Johirul uses internet banking to connect with "domestic and foreign" parties.
Analysis: Internet banking allows 24/7 fund transfers (EFT/RTGS), real-time account monitoring, and payment of import/export bills via the web.
Conclusion: He gains the benefits of speed, global reach, cost reduction, and the ability to manage finances without physical branch visits.

questions.label: d
questions.text: How effective is internet banking in expanding Mr. Johirul Islam's business? Analyze.
questions.answer:
Data: His business is "expanding day by day" due to modernization.
Analysis: Internet banking removes geographical boundaries, allowing him to trade with international suppliers and customers instantly.
Evaluation: It reduces the transaction lead time and improves the efficiency of working capital management.
Decision: Internet banking is highly effective; it provides the digital infrastructure necessary for a modern business to scale rapidly and maintain competitiveness in a global market.

fullCQData.stem: Mr. Hasan was provided a card by his bank. With this card, he can make various purchases and pay bills as long as there is money in his account. On the other hand, Mr. Zaman is using a card despite not having a bank account, which allows him to spend money up to a certain limit.

questions.label: a
questions.text: What is the full form of ATM?
questions.answer: The full form of ATM is Automated Teller Machine.

questions.label: b
questions.text: Why is mobile banking popular?
questions.answer: Mobile banking is popular because it offers 24/7 convenience, allows transactions from anywhere using a smartphone, and provides instant services like fund transfers and bill payments at a very low cost.

questions.label: c
questions.text: What type of card is used by Mr. Hasan in the stem? Explain.
questions.answer:
Data: Mr. Hasan can use the card "as long as there is money in his account."
Analysis: A card that only allows spending based on the existing account balance is a Debit Card.
Conclusion: Mr. Hasan is using a 'Debit Card'.

questions.label: d
questions.text: Mr. Zaman's facility from the bank has made it easier to obtain a loan. Give your opinion in light of the stem.
questions.answer:
Data: Mr. Zaman does not have a standard account but can "spend up to a certain limit."
Analysis: This is a 'Credit Card' or a specialized 'Pre-approved Credit' facility where the bank provides a loan limit.
Evaluation: Traditionally, getting a loan involves extensive paperwork and collateral. A credit card provides a pre-approved, instant loan without specific application for every transaction.
Decision: I agree with the statement. The credit card serves as a "perpetual loan" facility, giving Mr. Zaman immediate access to funds, which simplifies the process of borrowing during emergencies or for planned purchases.

fullCQData.stem: Shuvo and Sojib are two friends. They traveled from Khulna to Dhaka with a small amount of cash and two special magnetic cards provided by the bank. Using Shuvo's card, he can withdraw money deposited in his account at any time. On the other hand, Sojib's card allows him to make all purchases and pay bills even if he does not have sufficient funds in his account. However, Sojib has to pay back the excess withdrawn amount to the bank with interest later.

questions.label: a
questions.text: What is the full form of POS?
questions.answer: The full form of POS is Point of Sale.

questions.label: b
questions.text: Why is e-banking important for ensuring customer service?
questions.answer: E-banking is important because it ensures 24/7 banking access, reduces the need for physical branch visits, speeds up fund transfers, and provides real-time transaction updates, thereby increasing customer satisfaction and efficiency.

questions.label: c
questions.text: What is the name of the card used by Shuvo? Explain in light of the stem.
questions.answer:
Data: Shuvo can only withdraw money "deposited in his account."
Analysis: A magnetic card that allows transactions strictly within the limit of the existing bank balance is a Debit Card.
Conclusion: Shuvo's card is a 'Debit Card'.

questions.label: d
questions.text: Sojib's card is better than Shuvo's. Give arguments in favor of your answer.
questions.answer:
Data: Sojib has a Credit Card (loan facility) and Shuvo has a Debit Card.
Analysis: While traveling, unforeseen expenses may arise. A Credit Card provides emergency funds even when the bank balance is zero.
Evaluation: It acts as an instant loan, providing higher purchasing power and financial security in a new city.
Decision: Yes, Sojib's 'Credit Card' is superior in terms of flexibility and emergency support, especially during travel.

fullCQData.stem: Mr. Sakib is a businessman. Merchant bank authorities issued him a plastic card for transaction convenience, which he can use to withdraw cash and make purchases. However, for business needs, he failed to conduct a transaction exceeding his balance. After complaining to the bank, the bank issued him another new card, which he now uses for all purposes.

questions.label: a
questions.text: What is Electronic Banking?
questions.answer: Electronic Banking is a method of banking where transactions are conducted through electronic systems like the internet, mobile phones, or ATMs instead of physical paper-based methods.

questions.label: b
questions.text: Why is the use of ATMs so popular nowadays?
questions.answer: ATMs are popular because they provide 24/7 cash withdrawal facilities, allow balance inquiries and mini-statements at any location, and eliminate the need to wait in long bank queues during office hours.

questions.label: c
questions.text: What type of card was issued to Mr. Sakib by the bank? Explain.
questions.answer:
Data: Mr. Sakib could withdraw cash and shop but "failed to transact exceeding his balance."
Analysis: A card that restricts spending to the available account balance is a Debit Card.
Conclusion: The first card issued to Mr. Sakib was a 'Debit Card'.

questions.label: d
questions.text: How can Mr. Sakib's newly received card play a greater role in his business? Explain in light of the stem.
questions.answer:
Data: The new card allows him to overcome the balance restriction.
Analysis: The second card is a Credit Card, which offers a pre-approved credit limit (overdraft).
Evaluation: In business, sudden opportunities or urgent payments often require more cash than is currently in the account.
Decision: The new 'Credit Card' will allow Mr. Sakib to maintain business continuity, pay suppliers on time even during a cash crunch, and manage short-term working capital more effectively.

fullCQData.stem: Mr. Alam is an NGO official. He operates an account at 'Chitra Bank Ltd.,' which has a special plastic card with a coded number. With this card, he can withdraw deposited money and pay for goods. However, due to being extremely busy, he is facing problems in paying gas, water, and electricity bills regularly. He informed his bank about this. Understanding the importance, the bank manager encouraged Mr. Alam to receive banking services by registering on the bank's website with his name and a specific password.

questions.label: a
questions.text: What is the full form of SWIFT?
questions.answer: The full form of SWIFT is Society for Worldwide Interbank Financial Telecommunication.

questions.label: b
questions.text: What is meant by Point of Sale (POS)?
questions.answer: Point of Sale (POS) refers to the place and time where a retail transaction is completed, typically using a terminal that processes credit or debit card payments at a store counter.

questions.label: c
questions.text: Which plastic card did Mr. Alam receive from the bank in the stem? Explain.
questions.answer:
Data: Mr. Alam uses a card with a "coded number" (PIN) to withdraw "deposited money" and buy goods.
Analysis: A card linked to a bank account used for withdrawals and payments within the balance limit is a Debit Card.
Conclusion: Mr. Alam received a 'Debit Card'.

questions.label: d
questions.text: Do you think the advice given by the manager of 'Chitra Bank Ltd.' to Mr. Alam is capable of solving his problem? Give arguments in favor.
questions.answer:
Data: The manager advised him to use the "bank's website" with a "password."
Analysis: This refers to 'Internet Banking' or 'Online Banking'.
Evaluation: Internet banking allows users to pay utility bills (gas, water, electricity) instantly from home or office at any time of day.
Decision: Yes, the advice is highly effective. Internet banking will allow Mr. Alam to automate or manually pay his bills in seconds, solving his problem of time constraints and ensuring he never misses a payment deadline again.

fullCQData.stem: Mr. Azad opened a savings account at XYZ Bank. In addition to a checkbook, the bank provided him with a special card for urgent transactions. With it, he can shop, enjoy loan facilities, and buy goods on credit up to a certain limit. Mr. Azad withdraws money with the card using a secret number. For this, he has to use a magnetic machine located in a small room.

questions.label: a
questions.text: What is Home Banking?
questions.answer: Home Banking is a service that allows customers to perform banking transactions (such as balance inquiries or fund transfers) from their homes using a telephone or a computer.

questions.label: b
questions.text: What is meant by Electronic Banking?
questions.answer: Electronic Banking refers to the delivery of banking products and services through electronic interactive communication channels, such as the internet, mobile phones, or ATMs.

questions.label: c
questions.text: Explain the necessity of using a secret number for the card in the stem.
questions.answer: The secret number (PIN) is essential for security. It acts as a digital signature that authenticates the cardholder's identity, ensuring that even if the physical card is lost or stolen, no unauthorized person can withdraw money or make transactions.

questions.label: d
questions.text: Can Mr. Azad withdraw money using the card in the stem?
questions.answer:
Data: Mr. Azad uses a "magnetic machine in a small room" and a "secret number" with a card that offers "loan facilities."
Analysis: The machine is an ATM and the card is a Credit Card.
Conclusion: Yes, Mr. Azad can withdraw cash from an ATM using this card, although cash advances on credit cards often incur higher interest rates.

fullCQData.stem: Mr. Alam is an employee. When he opened a savings account at the 'B' branch of 'A' bank, the bank provided him with a checkbook. Additionally, for convenience in urgent transactions, the bank provided him with a special card. Using that card, he can withdraw money from ATM booths as well as complete all kinds of shopping. On the other hand, his friend Mr. Anis uses a card that allows him to complete all shopping in cash and on credit. Moreover, loan facilities are available with this type of card.

questions.label: a
questions.text: What is e-banking?
questions.answer: E-banking (Electronic Banking) is the automated delivery of banking services directly to customers through electronic communication channels.

questions.label: b
questions.text: Bank is the holder of borrowed funds—Explain.
questions.answer: Banks are called "holders of borrowed funds" because they collect deposits from savers (which is essentially borrowing from the public) and then lend that money to borrowers. The bank acts as a custodian of these borrowed sums.

questions.label: c
questions.text: What type of card is used by Mr. Alam in the stem? Explain.
questions.answer:
Data: Mr. Alam's card is linked to his "savings account" and allows him to spend or withdraw his own money.
Analysis: A card that enables transactions only within the limit of the account balance is a Debit Card.
Conclusion: Mr. Alam is using a 'Debit Card'.

questions.label: d
questions.text: Between the two cards used in the stem, which one seems better to you? Analyze.
questions.answer:
Data: Alam has a Debit Card; Anis has a card with "credit and loan facilities" (Credit Card).
Analysis: A Credit Card provides emergency funds and a "buy now, pay later" advantage, whereas a Debit Card requires an immediate balance.
Evaluation: While a Debit Card promotes discipline, a Credit Card is superior for managing unexpected financial crises.
Decision: The 'Credit Card' is better due to its versatility, global acceptance, and role as a short-term credit source.

fullCQData.stem: Mrs. Sonia sends money to her mother using the most popular and easily portable communication device. Additionally, she personally uses a plastic card that provides her with loan facilities.

questions.label: a
questions.text: What is a Debit Card?
questions.answer: A Debit Card is a plastic payment card that provides the cardholder electronic access to their bank account, allowing them to spend or withdraw money up to their available balance.

questions.label: b
questions.text: Why is the use of ATM cards so popular?
questions.answer: ATM cards are popular because they provide 24/7 access to cash, allow for quick balance inquiries, and eliminate the need to visit a bank branch or stand in long queues during limited banking hours.

questions.label: c
questions.text: Through which e-banking technology does Mrs. Sonia send money? Explain.
questions.answer:
Data: Mrs. Sonia uses a "popular and portable communication device" (smartphone) to send money.
Analysis: This refers to Mobile Banking or Mobile Financial Services (MFS) like bKash, Rocket, or Nagad.
Conclusion: Mrs. Sonia sends money through 'Mobile Banking'.

questions.label: d
questions.text: Mrs. Sonia's card reduces the use of paper currency—do you agree? Give your opinion.
questions.answer:
Data: She uses a plastic card with a loan facility (Credit Card).
Analysis: Plastic cards allow for digital payments at POS terminals and online, reducing the need to carry physical cash for large or daily purchases.
Evaluation: This shift toward a "cashless economy" improves security and transaction efficiency.
Decision: I agree. Such cards replace physical currency with digital data, significantly reducing the demand for and circulation of paper money.

fullCQData.stem: Samad and Rahat have accounts at Delta Bank. The bank provided them with two plastic cards. They went shopping at a mall. Even though they had no cash, they were able to shop using the plastic cards. In this case, Samad had money in his account, but Rahat did not have any money in his bank account.

questions.label: a
questions.text: What is online banking?
questions.answer: Online banking is a service provided by a bank that allows its customers to conduct financial transactions and monitor their accounts via the internet through a secure website.

questions.label: b
questions.text: What is meant by mobile banking?
questions.answer: Mobile banking is a service provided by a bank or other financial institution that allows its customers to conduct financial transactions remotely using a mobile device such as a smartphone or tablet.

questions.label: c
questions.text: Which plastic card of the bank enabled Rahat to shop? Explain.
questions.answer:
Data: Rahat was able to complete his shopping even though he had "no money in his bank account."
Analysis: A credit card allows a user to spend up to a pre-approved limit set by the bank, regardless of their current account balance.
Conclusion: Rahat was able to shop due to a 'Credit Card'.

questions.label: d
questions.text: Which of the two plastic cards mentioned in the stem has relatively more advantages and facilities? Analyze.
questions.answer:
Data: Samad used a Debit Card (linked to balance) and Rahat used a Credit Card (linked to a loan limit).
Analysis: A Credit Card offers emergency liquidity, reward points, and the ability to "buy now, pay later," whereas a Debit Card is strictly limited to existing savings.
Evaluation: In modern commerce, the ability to access funds during a cash crunch makes the credit card more versatile.
Decision: The 'Credit Card' has relatively more facilities because it functions both as a payment tool and a short-term credit source.

fullCQData.stem: A few years ago, if money was sent through a bank in Dhaka, it took several days to reach the recipient. Currently, if money is sent through a bank in Dhaka, the recipient receives it within a few minutes. No check is required.

questions.label: a
questions.text: What is a debit card?
questions.answer: A debit card is a plastic payment card that allows the holder to withdraw cash from an ATM or make payments at a POS terminal by directly deducting the amount from their bank account balance.

questions.label: b
questions.text: What is meant by mobile banking?
questions.answer: Mobile banking is a financial service that allows customers to access their bank accounts and perform transactions (like fund transfers and bill payments) instantly using a mobile app or USSD codes.

questions.label: c
questions.text: What type of banking is mentioned in the stem? Explain.
questions.answer:
Data: Money now reaches the recipient in "minutes" and "no check is required."
Analysis: The instant transfer of funds electronically across banks or branches is known as Electronic Fund Transfer (EFT) or Online Banking.
Conclusion: The stem refers to 'Online Banking' or 'Electronic Banking'.

questions.label: d
questions.text: Modern banking is more advanced than the banking system of the past.—Analyze in light of the stem.
questions.answer:
Data: Past banking was slow (days); modern banking is fast (minutes).
Analysis: Digitalization has replaced manual ledger entries with real-time processing, and physical checks with electronic transfers.
Evaluation: Modern banking offers 24/7 access, higher security, and extreme speed compared to the traditional branch-bound manual system.
Decision: The statement is valid; the modern electronic system has revolutionized efficiency, making financial transactions seamless and instantaneous.

fullCQData.stem: Mr. Suman is a successful businessman. He has a loan of fifty lakh BDT in his business, which must be paid in installments. He is currently failing to pay the loan installments. Meanwhile, his insured car worth thirty lakh BDT was damaged in an accident. When a claim was filed with the insurance company, the company paid full compensation.

questions.label: a
questions.text: What is insurance?
questions.answer: Insurance is a legal contract where one party (the insurer) agrees to compensate another party (the insured) for specific losses or damages in exchange for a fee called a premium.

questions.label: b
questions.text: Why is insurance called a contract of indemnity?
questions.answer: Insurance is called a contract of indemnity because its purpose is to restore the insured to the same financial position they were in before the loss occurred, without allowing them to make a profit from the misfortune.

questions.label: c
questions.text: What type of risk is included in Mr. Suman's inability to pay the loan installments? Explain.
questions.answer:
Data: Mr. Suman is failing to pay his "fifty lakh BDT" loan installments.
Analysis: The risk of a borrower being unable to meet their financial obligations to a lender is known as Credit Risk or Financial Risk.
Conclusion: This falls under 'Financial Risk' or 'Credit Risk'.

questions.label: d
questions.text: Is it logical for the insurance company to provide full compensation to Mr. Suman as mentioned in the stem? Analyze in light of insurance principles.
questions.answer:
Data: The car was "insured" and "damaged in an accident."
Analysis: According to the principle of indemnity and the principle of insurable interest, the insurer is bound to pay the actual loss suffered.
Evaluation: Since he had a valid policy and a loss occurred due to a covered peril (accident), he is entitled to compensation.
Decision: Yes, it is logical. According to the 'Principle of Indemnity', the company's full compensation is justified as it fulfills the contract to cover the financial loss of the asset.

fullCQData.stem: Runi and Ruhi are two sisters. They went shopping together at a shop named 'Taj Co.' Runi usually does not carry cash and uses a type of plastic card issued against her account. While paying, she found out that there was an insufficient balance in her account. Having no other choice, Ruhi used another type of card to pay for both of their purchases. This card has a loan facility.

questions.label: a
questions.text: What is a KYC form?
questions.answer: KYC stands for 'Know Your Customer.' it is a standard form used by banks to verify the identity, address, and source of income of a customer to prevent financial crimes.

questions.label: b
questions.text: What is meant by Point of Sale (POS) service? Explain.
questions.answer: Point of Sale (POS) service refers to an electronic payment system where a customer pays for goods or services at a retail location using a plastic card through a specialized terminal connected to the bank's network.

questions.label: c
questions.text: What kind of "checks" are the cards used by Runi and Ruhi called? Explain.
questions.answer:
Data: The sisters used plastic cards instead of paper.
Analysis: In modern banking, plastic cards (Debit/Credit) are often referred to as 'Plastic Checks' because they perform the same function as a paper check—transferring funds from the payer to the receiver—but in a digital and more secure format.
Conclusion: These cards are known as 'Plastic Checks'.

questions.label: d
questions.text: Ruhi's card is more advantageous than Runi's card.—Analyze the logic of this statement.
questions.answer:
Data: Runi's card failed due to "insufficient balance" (Debit Card), while Ruhi's card worked because of a "loan facility" (Credit Card).
Analysis: A Credit Card provides a pre-approved credit limit, allowing transactions even when the bank balance is zero, which is critical in emergencies.
Evaluation: It also offers rewards and international acceptance.
Decision: The statement is logical; Ruhi's 'Credit Card' is superior because it provides emergency liquidity and higher purchasing power that a 'Debit Card' lacks.

fullCQData.stem: Mr. Rahat is a retail trader. He buys goods from large marketing shops and sells them to customers. He uses electronic banking facilities to pay for the purchased goods, where payments are made directly through a magnetic card at a specific terminal. To get banking facilities easily and quickly, he operates an account in a bank where he can receive services from any branch through electronic banking.

questions.label: a
questions.text: What is SWIFT?
questions.answer: SWIFT (Society for Worldwide Interbank Financial Telecommunication) is a global messaging network that financial institutions use to securely transmit information and instructions through a standardized system of codes.

questions.label: b
questions.text: Why is mobile banking popular? Explain.
questions.answer: Mobile banking is popular because it offers 24/7 access to financial services from anywhere via a smartphone, allowing for instant fund transfers and bill payments without visiting a physical branch.

questions.label: c
questions.text: Which e-banking service does Mr. Rahat use to make payments in the stem? Explain.
questions.answer:
Data: Mr. Rahat pays "directly through a magnetic card at a specific terminal."
Analysis: This system is known as POS (Point of Sale). It allows traders to settle transactions instantly without physical cash.
Conclusion: Mr. Rahat uses 'POS (Point of Sale)' banking services.

questions.label: d
questions.text: Which e-banking service allows receiving banking services most easily and quickly? Analyze.
questions.answer:
Data: Mr. Rahat can access his account from "any branch."
Analysis: A system where a customer's data is centralized, allowing them to transact from any branch of the bank nationwide, is called 'Online Banking' or 'Core Banking'.
Evaluation: It eliminates the restriction of the "home branch" and saves time.
Decision: 'Online/Core Banking' is the service that ensures the fastest and easiest access to banking, as it provides total geographical flexibility within the bank's network.

fullCQData.stem: Mr. Rumi is an employee. He operates an account at the Motijheel branch of Delta Bank in Dhaka. For transaction convenience, the bank issued him a magnetic plastic card and a checkbook. He was in Dinajpur for official work and when he needed money, he withdrew it from the Dinajpur branch of Delta Bank.

questions.label: a
questions.text: What is One-Stop Service?
questions.answer: One-Stop Service is a banking facility where a customer can receive all their required services (deposits, withdrawals, etc.) from a single counter or official.

questions.label: b
questions.text: Why is mobile banking popular? Explain.
questions.answer: Mobile banking is popular due to its 24/7 availability, ease of use through smartphones, cost-efficiency, and the ability to perform instant peer-to-peer transfers and utility bill payments.

questions.label: c
questions.text: What type of card did the bank issue to Mr. Rumi? Explain.
questions.answer:
Data: Mr. Rumi was issued a "magnetic plastic card" to withdraw "money" from his account.
Analysis: Since he is an employee and has a standard account, the card used to access his own deposited funds at ATMs or branches is a Debit Card.
Conclusion: The bank issued him a 'Debit Card'.

questions.label: d
questions.text: Evaluate Mr. Rumi's banking activities from the perspective of modern banking.
questions.answer:
Data: He withdrew money from the "Dinajpur branch" while his account is in the "Motijheel branch."
Analysis: This is an example of 'Any Branch Banking' or 'Online Banking'.
Evaluation: In the past, he would have had to travel back to his home branch or use a slow manual clearing process.
Decision: Modern banking has turned the entire bank network into a single point of service; Mr. Rumi's ability to transact across cities proves the efficiency and seamless nature of 'Core Banking' systems.

fullCQData.stem: Mr. Johir went to Cox's Bazar with his family. Besides a small amount of cash, he took a type of plastic card with him. With this, he was able to withdraw money from the bank there and also pay hotel bills, food bills, and even shopping bills.

questions.label: a
questions.text: What is Retained Earnings?
questions.answer: Retained Earnings refers to the portion of a company's net profit that is kept aside for future investment or to meet contingencies rather than being distributed as dividends to shareholders.

questions.label: b
questions.text: What is meant by impersonal security?
questions.answer: Impersonal security refers to tangible assets like land, buildings, gold, or financial documents (shares/bonds) pledged as collateral for a loan, where the bank relies on the value of the asset rather than just the borrower's personal promise.

questions.label: c
questions.text: What is the name of the plastic card Mr. Johir took with him? Write a short note on this card.
questions.answer:
Data: Mr. Johir uses the card for withdrawals and bill payments.
Analysis: A card that allows an account holder to access their own deposited funds for cash or payments is a Debit Card.
Note: A Debit Card is an electronic card linked directly to a bank account. It facilitates cashless transactions and 24/7 ATM access, but only up to the limit of the balance available in the account.

questions.label: d
questions.text: Discuss the advantages and disadvantages of the mentioned plastic card.
questions.answer:
Advantages: 24/7 cash access, eliminates the risk of carrying large amounts of physical cash, no interest charges (since it's your own money), and helps in budget tracking.
Disadvantages: Requires an existing bank balance, risk of fraud if the PIN is stolen, and potential technical issues at POS terminals or ATMs. A Debit Card is the most practical tool for a traveler to manage existing funds safely.

fullCQData.stem: Mr. Jahid needed cash urgently and went to an ATM booth with his electronic card. As soon as he inserted the card, the monitor showed that there was insufficient money in his account, and the card was ejected. Later, when he visited a banker friend, he was told about a special card that allows him to withdraw a specific amount even if there is no money in his account. Finally, he obtained this special card from the bank.

questions.label: a
questions.text: What is e-banking?
questions.answer: E-banking (Electronic Banking) is a method of banking where transactions are conducted through electronic systems like the internet or ATMs instead of physical paper-based methods.

questions.label: b
questions.text: Why is the use of ATMs so popular today? Explain.
questions.answer: ATMs are popular because they provide 24/7 cash withdrawal facilities, allow balance inquiries and mini-statements at any location, and eliminate the need to wait in long bank queues during office hours.

questions.label: c
questions.text: Due to which card did Mr. Jahid fail to withdraw money the first time? Explain.
questions.answer:
Data: The card was ejected because there was "insufficient money" in the account.
Analysis: A Debit Card only works if there is a positive balance in the linked account. It does not provide credit.
Conclusion: Mr. Jahid failed because he was using a 'Debit Card'.

questions.label: d
questions.text: The card obtained on the advice of Mr. Jahid's friend is more advantageous than the previous card. Do you agree? Give your opinion.
questions.answer:
Data: The first card required a balance; the second card allows "withdrawing more than the balance."
Analysis: A Credit Card provides a pre-approved loan limit, acting as an instant source of credit during emergencies.
Evaluation: In a crisis where the bank account is empty, only a Credit Card can provide liquidity.
Decision: Yes, I agree. The Credit Card is more advantageous because it provides a safety net and financial flexibility that the Debit Card lacks.

fullCQData.stem: Mr. Shakil sends money to his mother using the most popular and easily portable communication device. Additionally, he personally uses a plastic card that provides him with a loan facility and allows him to shop without cash.

questions.label: a
questions.text: What is a Credit Card?
questions.answer: A Credit Card is a plastic card issued by a bank that allows the holder to borrow funds up to a pre-approved limit to make purchases or withdraw cash.

questions.label: b
questions.text: Why is e-banking becoming more popular today? Explain.
questions.answer: E-banking is popular because it saves time, offers 24/7 convenience, allows transactions from anywhere, reduces the cost of banking operations, and ensures faster fund transfers compared to traditional methods.

questions.label: c
questions.text: Through which e-banking technology does Mr. Shakil send money? Explain.
questions.answer:
Data: Mr. Shakil uses a "popular and portable communication device" (smartphone) to send money.
Analysis: Sending money via mobile apps or USSD codes is known as Mobile Banking or Mobile Financial Services (MFS).
Conclusion: Mr. Shakil sends money through 'Mobile Banking'.

questions.label: d
questions.text: Mr. Shakil's card reduces the use of paper currency—do you agree? Give your opinion.
questions.answer:
Data: He uses a card for shopping and loans (Credit Card).
Analysis: Plastic cards allow for digital payments at POS terminals and online, reducing the need to carry physical cash for transactions.
Evaluation: This encourages a cashless economy and reduces the printing and circulation costs of physical notes.
Decision: Yes, I agree. Digital payment tools like Credit Cards and Mobile Banking are effectively replacing the role of paper currency in modern financial ecosystems.

fullCQData.stem: Naoshi and Oishi are two friends. They always conduct transactions with the bank for business needs. Naoshi goes to the bank to withdraw money. However, the bank issued a card to Oishi, which she can use to withdraw money from any location. As a result, her business activities have gained momentum.

questions.label: a
questions.text: What is the full name of SWIFT?
questions.answer: The full name of SWIFT is Society for Worldwide Interbank Financial Telecommunication.

questions.label: b
questions.text: What is meant by POS? Explain.
questions.answer: POS (Point of Sale) refers to an electronic system that allows customers to make payments for goods or services at a retail location using a plastic card through a terminal, instantly debiting their account or using a credit limit.

questions.label: c
questions.text: What type of banking is mentioned regarding Naoshi in the stem? Explain.
questions.answer:
Data: Naoshi "goes to the bank" (physical branch) to withdraw money.
Analysis: The traditional system where a customer must physically visit a bank counter during office hours to perform transactions is known as Branch Banking or Traditional Banking.
Conclusion: Naoshi is using 'Traditional Branch Banking'.

questions.label: d
questions.text: Evaluate the appropriateness of the type of card issued to Oishi in the stem.
questions.answer:
Data: Oishi can withdraw money from "any location" using a "card."
Analysis: This refers to a Debit or Credit card used at ATMs and POS terminals.
Evaluation: Business moves fast; Oishi saves time by avoiding bank queues and can manage cash flow 24/7.
Decision: The card is highly appropriate. It provides Oishi with the mobility and speed necessary for modern business, allowing her to settle payments and access cash instantly regardless of banking hours.

fullCQData.stem: Mrs. Monalisa is a customer of the Sylhet branch of Mohona Bank. Her son is studying at a college in Dhaka. Due to a special need, her son suddenly required money urgently. She approached Mohona Bank to send money from the Sylhet branch to the Dhaka branch within a single day. The bank completed her transaction almost instantly.

questions.label: a
questions.text: What is a Credit Card?
questions.answer: A Credit Card is a plastic card issued by a bank that allows the holder to purchase goods or services on credit or withdraw cash up to a pre-approved limit.

questions.label: b
questions.text: Why is mobile banking popular? Explain.
questions.answer: Mobile banking is popular because it provides 24/7 access to banking services via smartphones, allows instant fund transfers, and is highly cost-effective and convenient for users.

questions.label: c
questions.text: Based on functions, what type of bank is the one Mrs. Monalisa transacted with? Explain.
questions.answer:
Data: Mohona Bank accepts deposits and facilitates fund transfers between branches for general customers.
Analysis: A bank that performs general banking functions like accepting deposits, providing loans, and facilitating payments for the public is a Commercial Bank.
Conclusion: Mohona Bank is a 'Commercial Bank'.

questions.label: d
questions.text: The type of electronic banking service Mrs. Monalisa received is not possible in the traditional banking system.—Verify the truth of this statement in light of the stem.
questions.answer:
Data: Money was sent from Sylhet to Dhaka "almost instantly."
Analysis: Traditional banking relied on manual clearing and physical mailing of documents, taking days. Modern 'Online Banking' or 'Any Branch Banking' uses a centralized server (Core Banking) to update records instantly.
Evaluation: Without digital connectivity, instant inter-city transfers are impossible.
Decision: The statement is true. The speed and efficiency of this transaction are direct results of E-banking (Online Banking), which the manual traditional system could never achieve.

fullCQData.stem: Mrs. Sonia sends money to her mother using the most popular and easily portable communication device. Additionally, she personally uses a plastic card that provides her with a loan facility.
fullCQData.meta: Jalalabad Cantonment Public School & College, Sylhet · 2026

questions.label: a
questions.text: What is a Debit Card?
questions.answer: A Debit Card is an electronic card issued by a bank that allows the holder to access their own deposited funds for cash withdrawals or payments.

questions.label: b
questions.text: Why is the use of ATM cards so popular?
questions.answer: ATM cards are popular because they provide 24/7 access to cash at various locations, eliminate the need for bank queues, and allow for quick balance inquiries and mini-statements.

questions.label: c
questions.text: Through which e-banking technology does Mrs. Sonia send money? Explain.
questions.answer:
Data: Mrs. Sonia uses a "popular and portable communication device" (smartphone) to send money.
Analysis: This technology is known as Mobile Banking or Mobile Financial Services (MFS), which allows fund transfers via apps or USSD codes.
Conclusion: Mrs. Sonia sends money through 'Mobile Banking'.

questions.label: d
questions.text: Mrs. Sonia's card reduces the use of paper currency—do you agree? Give your opinion.
questions.answer:
Data: She uses a card with a "loan facility" (Credit Card).
Analysis: Plastic cards allow for electronic settlement of transactions at shops (POS) and online.
Evaluation: When people use cards and mobile transfers, the need to carry, count, and exchange physical notes decreases, leading to a cashless economy.
Decision: I agree. Digital tools like Credit Cards and Mobile Banking are significantly reducing the dependency on physical paper currency.

fullCQData.stem: Mr. Sakib is a businessman. Merchant bank authorities issued him a plastic card for transaction convenience, which he can use to withdraw cash and make purchases. However, for business needs, he failed to conduct a transaction exceeding his balance. After complaining to the bank, the bank issued him another new card, which he now uses for all purposes.

questions.label: a
questions.text: What is Electronic Banking?
questions.answer: Electronic Banking is a system of banking where transactions are conducted through electronic interactive communication channels (like the internet or mobile devices) rather than traditional paper-based methods.

questions.label: b
questions.text: Why is the use of ATMs so popular nowadays?
questions.answer: ATMs are popular because they provide 24/7 access to cash, allow balance inquiries and mini-statements at any location, and eliminate the need to wait in long bank queues during office hours.

questions.label: c
questions.text: What type of card was issued to Mr. Sakib by the bank? Explain.
questions.answer:
Data: Mr. Sakib could withdraw cash and shop but "failed to transact exceeding his balance."
Analysis: A magnetic card that restricts spending to the actual amount available in the linked bank account is a Debit Card.
Conclusion: The first card issued to Mr. Sakib was a 'Debit Card'.

questions.label: d
questions.text: How can Mr. Sakib's newly received card play a greater role in his business? Explain in light of the stem.
questions.answer:
Data: The new card allows transactions that his previous card (limited to balance) could not handle.
Analysis: This is a Credit Card, which offers a pre-approved credit limit (overdraft facility).
Evaluation: In business, liquidity is key. A Credit Card allows Mr. Sakib to pay suppliers or meet urgent costs even when his cash flow is temporarily low.
Decision: The new 'Credit Card' is more effective as it provides a short-term loan facility, enhancing his purchasing power and ensuring business continuity.

fullCQData.stem: In foreign trade, a widely used credit instrument is employed so that neither the exporter nor the importer has to worry about receiving or paying the price of goods. Through this document, the bank takes responsibility for the importer's debt. In this, the bank guarantees the exporter on behalf of the importer that the price will be paid under certain conditions and promises to pay it itself in case of the importer's inability. It is playing an undeniable role in creating economic dynamism and overall development.

questions.label: a
questions.text: What is a Cash Credit?
questions.answer: Cash Credit is a short-term business loan facility where a borrower can withdraw funds up to a certain limit against the security of tangible assets or commodities.

questions.label: b
questions.text: Secured loans are safe for banks—Explain.
questions.answer: Secured loans are safe because the bank holds a claim over a specific asset (collateral). If the borrower fails to repay, the bank can sell the asset to recover the outstanding loan amount, minimizing financial loss.

questions.label: c
questions.text: Discuss the import-export activities through the credit instrument mentioned in the stem.
questions.answer:
Data: The document involves a bank "guaranteeing payment" on behalf of an importer to an exporter.
Analysis: This document is a Letter of Credit (L/C). In foreign trade, the L/C acts as the bridge of trust. The importer opens it at their bank, and the exporter ships goods knowing the bank will pay upon presentation of shipping documents.
Conclusion: The instrument is a 'Letter of Credit (L/C)'.

questions.label: d
questions.text: How do you think the document mentioned in the stem is helping in business and trade? Give your opinion.
questions.answer:
Data: The L/C ensures payment and creates economic dynamism.
Analysis: International trade involves high risk due to distance and different legal systems.
Evaluation: The L/C eliminates the risk of non-payment for the exporter and ensures the importer that money is only released once goods are shipped.
Decision: I believe the L/C is the backbone of global trade; it facilitates large-scale transactions, builds international trust, and allows businesses to expand beyond domestic borders safely.

fullCQData.stem: Suddenly, on a bank holiday, Mr. Mukit needed cash and went to a nearby ATM booth with his electronic card. As soon as he inserted the card and entered the password and amount, the card came out without any money. At that time, the message "insufficient funds" was visible on the monitor. When he went to his friend, the friend talked about a special type of card with which he often withdraws extra money without having a balance. He was able to withdraw money through that card.

questions.label: a
questions.text: What is Forfaiting?
questions.answer: Forfaiting is a financial transaction where an exporter sells their medium and long-term receivables (amounts owed by the importer) to a specialized finance firm (forfaiter) at a discount for immediate cash.

questions.label: b
questions.text: Insecurity is the main obstacle to electronic banking—Explain.
questions.answer: E-banking relies on digital networks. If these are hacked or compromised, customers risk losing their money and sensitive data. This fear of fraud and lack of robust cybersecurity often prevents people from fully trusting electronic systems.

questions.label: c
questions.text: Due to which card do you think Mr. Mukit failed to withdraw money the first time?
questions.answer:
Data: The machine showed "insufficient funds" and Mr. Mukit could not withdraw more than his balance.
Analysis: A Debit Card is an electronic card that only allows the user to access funds they have already deposited. It does not offer any credit.
Conclusion: Mr. Mukit failed because he was using a 'Debit Card'.

questions.label: d
questions.text: Give your opinion on the fact that the card used by Mr. Mukit's friend is more advantageous.
questions.answer:
Data: His friend's card allows "withdrawing extra money without having a balance."
Analysis: This is a Credit Card. It provides an instant loan up to a specific limit.
Evaluation: During bank holidays or emergencies when one's account is empty, a Credit Card acts as a vital safety net.
Decision: I agree that the 'Credit Card' is more advantageous in this scenario because it provides financial liquidity and emergency support that a 'Debit Card' cannot offer.

fullCQData.stem: Mr. Islam is an employee working in a reputable organization in Dhaka, far from his family. He wants to send money to his father every month for household expenses. It is difficult for his elderly father to travel repeatedly to withdraw money from the bank. Therefore, Islam sends money using a wireless telecommunication service of the e-banking system. On the other hand, Mr. Akbar is an established businessman who needs to complete many transactions quickly every day. For this, he uses the internet on his personal computer to access various bank account information from home.

questions.label: a
questions.text: What is Point of Sale (POS) service?
questions.answer: Point of Sale (POS) is an electronic payment system that allows customers to pay for goods or services at a retail store using a plastic card through a specialized terminal.

questions.label: b
questions.text: Automated Clearing House has simplified inter-bank transactions.—Explain.
questions.answer: Automated Clearing House (ACH) simplifies inter-bank transactions by electronically processing high-volume batches of credit and debit transfers (like salaries or bills) automatically, eliminating the need for physical check movement and manual labor.

questions.label: c
questions.text: Through which e-banking service does Mr. Islam send money to his father? Explain.
questions.answer:
Data: Mr. Islam uses a "wireless telecommunication service" to send money so his father doesn't have to visit a bank.
Analysis: Banking services accessed via mobile devices and telecommunication networks are known as Mobile Banking.
Conclusion: Mr. Islam uses 'Mobile Banking' (MFS) services like bKash, Rocket, or Nagad.

questions.label: d
questions.text: What type of banking service is Mr. Akbar receiving in the stem? Analyze.
questions.answer:
Data: Mr. Akbar uses a "personal computer" and "internet" to manage his account from home.
Analysis: Banking activities performed over the internet through a bank’s secure website is called Internet Banking.
Evaluation: It provides 24/7 access to account statements and fund transfers without physical branch visits.
Decision: Mr. Akbar is receiving 'Internet Banking' services, which are essential for his large-scale daily business transactions.

fullCQData.stem: Koli and Pushpo are colleagues working in the same organization. After office, they went shopping together. Koli paid for her purchases using an electronic card. On the other hand, when Pushpo tried to pay with her electronic card, she found out there were insufficient funds in her account. Koli then informed Pushpo about her card, saying that it can be used for shopping even if there is no money in the account. Even having a bank account is not mandatory to use this card.

questions.label: a
questions.text: What is SWIFT?
questions.answer: SWIFT (Society for Worldwide Interbank Financial Telecommunication) is a global network that allows financial institutions to securely send and receive information about financial transactions in a standardized format.

questions.label: b
questions.text: Why is the use of ATMs so popular today? Explain.
questions.answer: ATMs are popular because they provide 24/7 cash withdrawal facilities at various locations, allow for quick balance inquiries, and save customers from waiting in long bank queues.

questions.label: c
questions.text: What type of card is used by Pushpo in the stem? Explain.
questions.answer:
Data: Pushpo’s card transaction failed because there was "insufficient money" in her account.
Analysis: A card that only allows transactions within the limit of the existing account balance is a Debit Card.
Conclusion: Pushpo is using a 'Debit Card'.

questions.label: d
questions.text: “Shopping can be done even if there is no money in the account.”—Analyze the validity of Koli’s statement.
questions.answer:
Data: Koli says the card works with "no balance" and a traditional bank account is "not mandatory."
Analysis: This refers to a Credit Card (loan-based) or a Prepaid Card.
Evaluation: Credit cards provide a pre-approved loan limit regardless of the user's cash balance.
Decision: Koli’s statement is valid; a 'Credit Card' acts as a source of instant credit, allowing purchases today and payment later, which is highly effective during a cash crunch.

fullCQData.stem: Mr. Abbas operates a savings account at Dutch-Bangla Bank Ltd. The bank provided him with a card through which he enjoys 24/7 banking facilities. On the other hand, Mr. Rashed is an employee. A bank representative visited his workplace and requested him to take a credit card, which he accepted. The bank set a limit for cash withdrawals. Mr. Rashed can shop by withdrawing that amount, which he pays back later.

questions.label: a
questions.text: What is a Debit Card?
questions.answer: A debit card is a plastic card issued by a bank that allows the holder to withdraw cash or make payments by directly deducting the amount from their existing bank account balance.

questions.label: b
questions.text: Why is the use of ATMs so popular today?
questions.answer: ATMs are popular because they offer 24/7 accessibility, reduce the need for physical visits to a bank branch, and provide secure and fast cash withdrawal and balance inquiry services.

questions.label: c
questions.text: What type of card is used by Mr. Abbas in the stem? Explain.
questions.answer:
Data: Mr. Abbas has a "savings account" and uses the card for "24/7 banking facilities."
Analysis: A card linked to a savings account for 24/7 ATM/POS access is a Debit Card.
Conclusion: Mr. Abbas is using a 'Debit Card'.

questions.label: d
questions.text: “Between the two cards mentioned in the stem, which one is better?”—Present your justification.
questions.answer:
Data: Abbas has a Debit Card; Rashed has a Credit Card (loan limit/pay later).
Analysis: A Debit Card uses personal savings (no debt), while a Credit Card offers emergency funds and credit history building.
Evaluation: For financial discipline, a Debit Card is better. For emergency liquidity and "buy now pay later" benefits, a Credit Card is superior.
Decision: The 'Credit Card' is often considered better in modern contexts as it provides an emergency financial safety net that the Debit Card does not.

fullCQData.stem: Mr. Rafiq recently started using a type of card for withdrawing money and paying loan installments. However, this card has no functionality if there is insufficient money in the bank account. On the other hand, his brother Shafiq uses another type of card which acts as an alternative to cash; he can purchase goods or services and withdraw cash up to a certain amount even if there is no money in the account.

questions.label: a
questions.text: What is the full form of SWIFT?
questions.answer: The full form of SWIFT is Society for Worldwide Interbank Financial Telecommunication.

questions.label: b
questions.text: What is meant by Point of Sale (POS)?
questions.answer: Point of Sale (POS) is an electronic system that allows customers to make payments for goods or services at a retail location using a magnetic card through a terminal, instantly updating the bank records.

questions.label: c
questions.text: What type of card does Rafiq use in the stem? Explain.
questions.answer:
Data: Rafiq's card has "no functionality if there is insufficient money" in the account.
Analysis: A card that is strictly linked to the existing bank balance and does not allow spending beyond that limit is a Debit Card.
Conclusion: Mr. Rafiq is using a 'Debit Card'.

questions.label: d
questions.text: Between the cards used by Rafiq and Shafiq, which one is better for 24/7 and instant transactions? Explain.
questions.answer:
Data: Rafiq uses a Debit Card; Shafiq uses a card that works "without a balance" (Credit Card).
Analysis: While both cards offer 24/7 ATM and POS access, a Credit Card provides an extra layer of emergency liquidity when personal savings are exhausted.
Evaluation: Shafiq can handle unplanned emergencies instantly, whereas Rafiq is limited by his current savings.
Decision: 'Shafiq's Credit Card' is superior for instant and 24/7 transactions as it ensures payment capability regardless of the account's current cash status.

fullCQData.stem: The customers of Kaptai Bank Ltd. can perform all types of banking activities through any of its branches. Recently, in a meeting of the bank's board of directors, a decision was taken to bring the unbanked population under banking services. Consequently, the Managing Director took an initiative to provide banking services through mobile technology.

questions.label: a
questions.text: What is Point of Sale (POS)?
questions.answer: Point of Sale (POS) is the time and place where a retail transaction is completed using an electronic terminal to process card payments.

questions.label: b
questions.text: Do customers get loan facilities through a credit card? Explain.
questions.answer: Yes, customers get loan facilities through a credit card. It allows the holder to spend money up to a certain pre-approved limit, which is essentially a short-term, revolving loan from the bank.

questions.label: c
questions.text: What type of electronic banking facility is Kaptai Bank Ltd. providing to its customers? Explain.
questions.answer:
Data: Customers can perform activities "through any of its branches."
Analysis: A system where a customer's account is accessible from any branch of a bank network is known as 'Online Banking' or 'Any Branch Banking'.
Conclusion: Kaptai Bank Ltd. is providing 'Online Banking' services.

questions.label: d
questions.text: Is the initiative of the bank's Managing Director to provide banking services appropriate? Express your opinion.
questions.answer:
Data: The initiative uses "mobile technology" to reach "unbanked populations."
Analysis: Mobile banking reaches rural areas where setting up physical branches is expensive.
Evaluation: It provides a low-cost, 24/7 banking solution for people who only have access to mobile phones.
Decision: Yes, the initiative is highly appropriate. It promotes financial inclusion, increases the bank's customer base, and helps in the overall economic development of the country.

fullCQData.stem: Ms. Rubin is an established businesswoman. She operates an account at 'Chitra Bank'. To provide banking facilities easily and quickly, the bank authority installed software on her personal computer so she can access all information about her account and pay various bills. Additionally, 'Chitra Bank' provided her with an electronic card that she can use as long as there is money in her account. Later, when she wanted to use the card for going abroad, the bank official advised her to take another card that can be used even if there is no money in the account.

questions.label: a
questions.text: What is SWIFT?
questions.answer: SWIFT (Society for Worldwide Interbank Financial Telecommunication) is a global messaging network that financial institutions use to securely transmit information and instructions through a standardized system of codes.

questions.label: b
questions.text: Why is it important to use a PIN in an ATM card? Explain.
questions.answer: A PIN (Personal Identification Number) is a secret numeric password that authenticates the user's identity. It is crucial because it ensures that even if the physical card is lost or stolen, unauthorized persons cannot access the funds or perform transactions.

questions.label: c
questions.text: What type of e-banking service did Ms. Rubin first receive in the stem? Explain.
questions.answer:
Data: The bank installed software on Ms. Rubin's "personal computer" for account access and bill payments.
Analysis: Banking services accessed via the internet through a dedicated website or software on a computer are known as Internet Banking.
Conclusion: Ms. Rubin first received 'Internet Banking' services.

questions.label: d
